operating_state: Nike achieved record revenues of $51.2 billion in fiscal 2023, increasing 10% reported and 16% currency-neutral, driven by broad-based growth across North America and EMEA while Greater China declined. Nike Direct revenues grew 14% representing 44% of total Nike Brand revenues, with digital sales and comparable store sales both increasing substantially driven by strategic pricing actions and favorable product mix shifts.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 250 basis points to 43.5% primarily due to higher product input costs, elevated freight and logistics expenses, increased promotional activity to liquidate excess inventory, and unfavorable foreign currency impacts. Total selling and administrative expenses increased 11% driven by higher wage-related costs, Nike Direct variable expenses from new store additions, strategic technology investments, and increased advertising spend.
material_events: Nike completed sales of entities in Chile, Argentina, and Uruguay to third-party distributors, finalizing the transition from wholesale and direct-to-consumer to distributor models in Central and South America. The company executed significant senior leadership restructuring effective May 24, 2023, with new appointments to President of Consumer, Product & Brand and Managing Director of Strategic Business Ventures. Nike returned $7.5 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends under the new four-year $18 billion program.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency fluctuations reduced reported revenues by approximately $2.9 billion and income before taxes by $1.0 billion net of hedges from U.S. Dollar strength. COVID-19 restrictions in Greater China caused temporary store closures and reduced retail traffic during the first two quarters before restrictions lifted. Supply chain volatility and inflationary pressures on input costs, freight, and logistics negatively impacted margins while the company monitored potential consumer behavior impacts from inflation and rising interest rates.
forward_outlook: Management expects the operating environment to remain volatile in fiscal 2024 with risks that worsening macroeconomic conditions could materially impact future revenue growth and profitability. The transition to distributor models in Central and South America is expected to reduce consolidated revenue growth due to different commercial terms but improve overall profitability through reduced expenses and lower foreign exchange exposure.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing examination of tax returns by the IRS and other authorities, with the European Commission investigating Netherlands tax rulings that could result in additional assessments. Information technology system vulnerabilities pose ongoing risks to supply chain operations and digital commerce capabilities, requiring continued security investments amid climate change regulations that may necessitate additional compliance costs.
